prompt: Implement a Python function `word_ladder_length(begin, end, word_list)` that returns the number of words in the SHORTEST transformation sequence from `begin` to `end`, where each step changes exactly one letter and every intermediate word (and `end`) must appear in `word_list`. All words are the same length and lowercase a-z. The returned count includes both `begin` and `end` (e.g. hit -> hot -> dot -> dog -> cog is length 5). `begin` itself need not be in `word_list`, but `end` must be in `word_list` for a sequence to exist. If `begin` equals `end`, return 1. If no transformation sequence exists, return 0. Do not mutate `word_list`. Use an efficient breadth-first search. Return ONLY a single ```python fenced code block containing the function definition and any helpers, and nothing else.

grading.tests:
assert word_ladder_length("hit", "cog", ["hot","dot","dog","lot","log","cog"]) == 5
assert word_ladder_length("hit", "cog", ["hot","dot","dog","lot","log"]) == 0
assert word_ladder_length("a", "c", ["a","b","c"]) == 2
assert word_ladder_length("hit", "hit", ["hit"]) == 1
assert word_ladder_length("hot", "dog", ["hot","dog"]) == 0
assert word_ladder_length("red", "tax", ["ted","tex","red","tax","tad","den","rex","pee"]) == 4
assert word_ladder_length("game", "code", ["game","gace","gcode","code"]) == 0
_wl = ["hot","dot","dog","lot","log","cog"]
assert word_ladder_length("hit", "cog", _wl) == 5 and _wl == ["hot","dot","dog","lot","log","cog"], 'must not mutate input'
assert word_ladder_length("abc", "abc", ["abc","abd"]) == 1
assert word_ladder_length("cat", "dog", ["cat","cot","cog","dog","dot"]) == 4
